name: Zosteropidae
description: White-eyes are a large family of small passerine birds found across Africa, Asia, and Australasia. They are named for the distinctive white eye-ring that most species possess and are highly active, social birds.

children.name: Zosterops
children.description: The largest genus of white-eyes, with over 100 species found across the Old World tropics.

children.name: Zosterops albogularis
children.description: A large white-eye with a broad white breast extending to the belly, olive-green upperparts, and a bold white eye-ring. It is endemic to Norfolk Island and is considered critically endangered, possibly extinct, due to habitat loss and introduced predators.

children.name: Zosterops aldabrensis
children.description: Endemic to Aldabra Atoll in the Seychelles, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, pale grey underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits scrub and mangroves across the atoll.

children.name: Zosterops anomalus
children.description: A distinctive white-eye endemic to Sulawesi, with a bright lemon-yellow throat and vent, olive upperparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits lowland forests and forest edges.

children.name: Zosterops atriceps
children.description: Found in the Moluccas of Indonesia, this white-eye has a dark-grey to blackish head, olive-green upperparts, and yellowish underparts. It inhabits lowland forests and coconut plantations.

children.name: Zosterops atrifrons
children.description: A white-eye from Sulawesi and adjacent islands with a distinctive black forehead and lores, olive-green upperparts, and pale yellow underparts. It is common in montane and lowland forests.

children.name: Zosterops atricapilla
children.description: A distinctive white-eye with a black crown and forehead, olive-green upperparts, and white underparts. Found in the mountains of Sumatra, Borneo, and the Malay Peninsula.

children.name: Zosterops auriventer
children.description: A white-eye found from Myanmar through Southeast Asia to Sumatra and Borneo. It has olive-green upperparts, a yellow throat and vent, and a bold white eye-ring, with a distinctive pale yellow belly.

children.name: Zosterops borbonicus
children.description: Endemic to Reunion Island, this white-eye has grey upperparts, whitish underparts, and a bold white eye-ring. It is common in forests and gardens across the island.

children.name: Zosterops brunneus
children.description: Endemic to the island of Biak in Papua, Indonesia, this white-eye has warm brown upperparts, pale underparts, and a distinctive white eye-ring. It inhabits lowland forests and is threatened by habitat loss.

children.name: Zosterops buruensis
children.description: Endemic to Buru Island in the Moluccas, this white-eye has bright yellow underparts, olive-green upperparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane and lowland forests.

children.name: Zosterops ceylonensis
children.description: Endemic to the highlands of Sri Lanka, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, bright yellow throat and breast, and a prominent white eye-ring. It is common in montane forests and gardens above 600 metres.

children.name: Zosterops chloris
children.description: Found from Sulawesi through the Lesser Sundas to northern Australia. It has bright lemon-yellow underparts, olive-green upperparts, and a distinct white eye-ring.

children.name: Zosterops chloronothos
children.description: Endemic to Mauritius, this white-eye has dull olive-green upperparts, pale grey underparts, and a thin white eye-ring. It is critically endangered, with a tiny remaining population in upland forests.

children.name: Zosterops chrysolaemus
children.description: Found in New Guinea and the Bismarck Archipelago, this white-eye has a black head, olive-green upperparts, and bright yellow underparts. It inhabits lowland forests and forest edges.

children.name: Zosterops cinereus
children.description: Endemic to Kosrae in the Caroline Islands of Micronesia, this white-eye has grey upperparts, whitish underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It is common in forests and gardens across the island.

children.name: Zosterops citrinella
children.description: A small white-eye found from the Lesser Sundas through the Moluccas to northern Australia. It has olive-green upperparts, pale grey underparts, and a white eye-ring.

children.name: Zosterops consobrinorum
children.description: Endemic to Sulawesi in Indonesia, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, yellow throat and vent, and a distinctive white eye-ring. It inhabits lowland forests and forest edges.

children.name: Zosterops conspicillatus
children.description: A white-eye native to the Mariana Islands in the western Pacific, with a distinctive black line through the eye giving it a 'bridled' appearance. It has olive-green upperparts and pale grey underparts.

children.name: Zosterops dehaani
children.description: Endemic to Biak and Supiori islands in Papua, Indonesia, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, pale yellow underparts, and a bold white eye-ring. It inhabits lowland tropical forests.

children.name: Zosterops erythropleurus
children.description: The Chestnut-flanked White-eye has olive upperparts, white underparts, and distinctive chestnut flanks. It breeds in temperate forests of East Asia and migrates to Southeast Asia for winter.

children.name: Zosterops eurycricotus
children.description: Found in the highlands of Tanzania and Kenya, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, pale grey underparts, and an exceptionally broad white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests and heathland.

children.name: Zosterops everetti
children.description: A white-eye found from the southern Philippines to Sulawesi and the Lesser Sundas. It has olive-green upperparts, a yellow throat and vent, and a bold white eye-ring.

children.name: Zosterops explorator
children.description: Endemic to Fiji, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, bright yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It is common in forests and gardens across the Fijian archipelago, often in noisy, active flocks.

children.name: Zosterops feae
children.description: Endemic to the island of Sao Tome in the Gulf of Guinea, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits forests and plantations across the island.

children.name: Zosterops ficedulinus
children.description: Endemic to Principe Island in the Gulf of Guinea, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, pale grey underparts, and a distinctive white eye-ring. It is found in forests and plantations.

children.name: Zosterops finschii
children.description: A small white-eye endemic to Palau in the western Pacific, with olive-green upperparts, yellow underparts, and a distinct white eye-ring. It is common in forests and mangroves throughout the Palau archipelago.

children.name: Zosterops flavifrons
children.description: Endemic to Vanuatu in the southwest Pacific, this white-eye has a bright yellow forehead and underparts, olive-green upperparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It is the most widespread and common land bird in Vanuatu.

children.name: Zosterops flavissimus
children.description: Found on the Wakatobi Islands of Sulawesi, this white-eye has brilliant yellow underparts, olive-green upperparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits lowland forests and scrub.

children.name: Zosterops fuscicapilla
children.description: Found in the mountains of New Guinea, this white-eye has a grey hood, olive-green upperparts, and pale yellow underparts. It inhabits montane forests and forest edges.

children.name: Zosterops goodfellowi
children.description: Endemic to montane forests of Mindanao in the Philippines, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, bright yellow underparts, and a bold white eye-ring. It forages actively in the forest canopy.

children.name: Zosterops grandis
children.description: Endemic to the island of New Guinea, this is one of the largest white-eye species with olive-green upperparts, pale yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits lowland forests.

children.name: Zosterops grayi
children.description: Endemic to the Kai Islands of Indonesia, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, pearly white underparts, and a distinctive white eye-ring. It inhabits lowland forests.

children.name: Zosterops griseotinctus
children.description: Found on islands from New Guinea to the Louisiade Archipelago, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, pale grey underparts, and a bold white eye-ring. It inhabits forests and mangroves.

children.name: Zosterops griseovirescens
children.description: Endemic to the island of Annobon in the Gulf of Guinea, this white-eye has greyish-olive upperparts, pale underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It is common in forests and scrub across the island.

children.name: Zosterops halmaheranus
children.description: Endemic to Halmahera in the Moluccas of Indonesia, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, yellow underparts, and a bold white eye-ring. It inhabits lowland and montane forests.

children.name: Zosterops hamlini
children.description: Endemic to Ranongga Island in the Solomon Islands, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, bright yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It is common in forests and gardens on the island.

children.name: Zosterops inornatus
children.description: Endemic to Lifou Island in New Caledonia, this white-eye is relatively large with olive-green upperparts, pale grey underparts, and a bold white eye-ring. It inhabits forests and scrub.

children.name: Zosterops japonicus
children.description: The Japanese White-eye is a small olive-green bird with a prominent white eye-ring and yellowish throat. It is common throughout East Asia and has become invasive in Hawaii.

children.name: Zosterops kaffensis
children.description: Found in the highlands of southwestern Ethiopia, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, pale yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests and bamboo thickets.

children.name: Zosterops kikuyuensis
children.description: Found in the highlands of central Kenya, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, pale grey underparts, and a bold white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests, bamboo, and gardens.

children.name: Zosterops kirki
children.description: Endemic to the Comoros Islands, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It is common in forests and plantations across the archipelago.

children.name: Zosterops kulalensis
children.description: Endemic to Mount Kulal in northern Kenya, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, pale underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests and juniper woodlands.

children.name: Zosterops lacertosus
children.description: Endemic to Nendo Island in the Solomon Islands, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, bright yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits lowland forests.

children.name: Zosterops lateralis
children.description: The Silvereye is a small, olive-green white-eye with a grey back and a distinctive white eye-ring. It is one of Australia's most common garden birds and also occurs in New Zealand and the southwest Pacific.

children.name: Zosterops luteus
children.description: A small white-eye endemic to coastal northern Australia, with bright yellow underparts, olive-green upperparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits mangroves, paperbark swamps, and coastal woodlands, often in noisy flocks.

children.name: Zosterops luteirostris
children.description: Endemic to the Solomon Islands, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, yellow underparts, and a distinctive yellow bill and prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits lowland forests.

children.name: Zosterops maderaspatanus
children.description: Found across Madagascar and nearby islands, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, pale grey underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It is common in forests, scrub, and gardens throughout its range.

children.name: Zosterops mayottensis
children.description: Endemic to Mayotte in the Comoros Islands, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, bright yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It is common in forests and gardens across the island.

children.name: Zosterops mbuluensis
children.description: Found in the highlands of Tanzania, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, pale grey underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests and heathland.

children.name: Zosterops meeki
children.description: Endemic to Tagula Island in Papua New Guinea, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits lowland forests.

children.name: Zosterops meratusensis
children.description: Endemic to the Meratus Mountains of Borneo, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, pale yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests.

children.name: Zosterops meyeni
children.description: Found in the northern Philippines and Taiwan, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, yellowish underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits lowland forests, scrub, and gardens.

children.name: Zosterops minor
children.description: Found in New Guinea, this white-eye has a narrow black forehead, olive-green upperparts, and pale yellow underparts. It inhabits montane forests and forest edges.

children.name: Zosterops mizanensis
children.description: Found in the highlands of southwestern Ethiopia, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, pale yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests and bamboo.

children.name: Zosterops modestus
children.description: Endemic to the Seychelles, this small white-eye has olive-green upperparts, pale grey underparts, and a thin white eye-ring. It is critically endangered, with a tiny population on a few islands.

children.name: Zosterops montanus
children.description: A highland white-eye found from Sumatra and Java through Sulawesi to the Philippines and New Guinea. It has dull olive-green upperparts, pale grey underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring.

children.name: Zosterops mouroniensis
children.description: Endemic to Mount Karthala on Grande Comore in the Comoros Islands, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests.

children.name: Zosterops murielae
children.description: Endemic to Vanikoro Island in the Solomon Islands, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, bright yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits lowland forests.

children.name: Zosterops mysorensis
children.description: Endemic to Biak Island in Papua, Indonesia, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits lowland forests.

children.name: Zosterops natalis
children.description: Endemic to Christmas Island in the Indian Ocean, this white-eye has bright olive-yellow upperparts, a yellow throat and vent, and a prominent white eye-ring. It is abundant in forests and gardens across the island.

children.name: Zosterops nehrkorni
children.description: Endemic to Sangihe Island off Sulawesi, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, bright yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It is critically endangered, with a tiny remaining population.

children.name: Zosterops nigrorum
children.description: Endemic to the Philippines, with bright yellow underparts, olive-green upperparts, and a white eye-ring. It is common in forests and gardens across many Philippine islands.

children.name: Zosterops novaeguineae
children.description: Found across much of New Guinea, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, pale yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits lowland and hill forests.

children.name: Zosterops obbiensis
children.description: Found in coastal Somalia, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, pale underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits dry scrub and coastal vegetation.

children.name: Zosterops olivaceus
children.description: Endemic to Reunion Island, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, pale yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It is common in forests and gardens across the island.

children.name: Zosterops pallidus
children.description: Found in southern Africa, this white-eye has pale olive-green upperparts, greyish-white underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits dry savanna and scrub.

children.name: Zosterops poliogastrus
children.description: Found in the highlands of Ethiopia and Eritrea, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, pale grey underparts, and a broad white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests and gardens.

children.name: Zosterops rendovae
children.description: Found in the Solomon Islands, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, a grey throat, pale underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests.

children.name: Zosterops rodericanus
children.description: Endemic to Rodrigues Island in the Mascarene Islands, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, pale grey underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It is common in forests and gardens.

children.name: Zosterops rotensis
children.description: Endemic to Rota in the Mariana Islands, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, pale yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It is critically endangered due to habitat loss and invasive species.

children.name: Zosterops samoensis
children.description: Endemic to the Samoan Islands, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, bright yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits forests and plantations, often in active mixed-species flocks.

children.name: Zosterops santaecrucis
children.description: Endemic to the Santa Cruz Islands in the Solomon Islands, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, bright yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits lowland forests.

children.name: Zosterops semperi
children.description: A bright yellow white-eye found on islands in Micronesia, including Palau and the Caroline Islands. It has vivid yellow underparts, olive-green upperparts, and a well-defined white eye-ring.

children.name: Zosterops senegalensis
children.description: The African Yellow White-eye is a small, bright yellow-olive bird with a conspicuous white eye-ring. It is widespread across sub-Saharan Africa in woodlands and savannas.

children.name: Zosterops silvanus
children.description: Endemic to the Taita Hills of Kenya, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, pale grey underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It is threatened by deforestation and habitat fragmentation.

children.name: Zosterops splendidus
children.description: Endemic to Ganongga Island in the Solomon Islands, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, bright golden-yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits lowland forests.

children.name: Zosterops stalkeri
children.description: Endemic to the Sula Islands of Indonesia, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits lowland forests.

children.name: Zosterops stierlingi
children.description: Found in the highlands of Tanzania and Malawi, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, pale yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests.

children.name: Zosterops strenuus
children.description: A large, robust white-eye endemic to Lord Howe Island, now extinct. It had olive-green upperparts, yellowish underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring, and was last recorded in the early 20th century.

children.name: Zosterops taiwanus
children.description: Endemic to Taiwan, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It is common in lowland forests and gardens across the island.

children.name: Zosterops tephropleurus
children.description: Endemic to Lord Howe Island in the Tasman Sea, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, pale grey underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It is common in forests and gardens across the island.

children.name: Zosterops uropygialis
children.description: Endemic to Timor and nearby islands in the Lesser Sundas, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, bright yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits lowland and montane forests.

children.name: Zosterops vaughani
children.description: Endemic to Pemba Island off the coast of Tanzania, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, a pale yellow belly, and a broad white eye-ring. It is common in forests, plantations, and gardens on the island.

children.name: Zosterops vellalavella
children.description: Endemic to Vella Lavella Island in the Solomon Islands, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, bright yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits lowland forests.

children.name: Zosterops virens
children.description: The Cape White-eye is a small, bright olive-green bird with a yellow throat and a bold white eye-ring. It is common in gardens and forests across southern Africa.

children.name: Zosterops wallacei
children.description: Found on islands in the Lesser Sundas and Moluccas, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, bright yellow underparts, and a yellow eye-ring that merges with the yellow forehead. It inhabits lowland forests.

children.name: Zosterops winifredae
children.description: Endemic to the South Pare Mountains of Tanzania, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, pale yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests.

children.name: Zosterops xanthochroa
children.description: Endemic to New Caledonia, this white-eye has bright olive-green upperparts, pale yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It is common in forests and scrub across the island.

children.name: Zosterops griseivirens
children.description: Found in the highlands of Tanzania, this white-eye has greyish-olive upperparts, pale underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests and scrub.

children.name: Zosterops leucogrammicus
children.description: Found in the mountains of Sumatra, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, white underparts with fine dark streaks, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests.

children.name: Zosterops melanogaster
children.description: Found in the mountains of Sumatra and Java, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts and a distinctive dark grey to black belly. It inhabits montane forests above 1000 metres.

children.name: Zosterops albiventris
children.description: Found in the mountains of Borneo, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, pure white underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests.

children.name: Zosterops stenocricotus
children.description: Found in the lowland forests of West and Central Africa, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, bright yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits rainforest canopies.

children.name: Zosterops mauritianus
children.description: Endemic to Mauritius, this white-eye has grey upperparts, white underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It is common in forests and gardens across the island, unlike the critically endangered Mauritius Olive White-eye.

children.name: Zosterops melanurus
children.description: Found in Java and Bali, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, pale grey underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring with a distinctive dark tail. It inhabits lowland forests and gardens.

children.name: Zosterops flavoviridis
children.description: Found in the Moluccas of Indonesia, this white-eye has bright yellow-green upperparts, yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits lowland forests.

children.name: Zosterops limbatus
children.description: Found in the Lesser Sunda Islands, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, pale grey underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits forests and scrub.

children.name: Zosterops platysternus
children.description: Found in the Philippines, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits lowland forests and scrub.

children.name: Zosterops chlorichroa
children.description: Found in the mountains of New Guinea, this white-eye has bright olive-green upperparts, pale green underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests.

children.name: Zosterops kasaicus
children.description: Found in the Kasai region of the Democratic Republic of the Congo, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, pale yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits forests.

children.name: Zosterops pallidiventris
children.description: Found in East Africa, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, pale whitish underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits coastal forests and scrub.

children.name: Zosterops capensis
children.description: Found in coastal South Africa, this white-eye has bright yellow underparts, olive-green upperparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits coastal scrub and gardens.

children.name: Zosterops andamanensis
children.description: Endemic to the Andaman Islands, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, bright yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It is common in forests across the archipelago.

children.name: Zosterops erythrothorax
children.description: Found in the highlands of Cameroon, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, a distinctive chestnut breast band, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests.

children.name: Zosterops cameroonensis
children.description: Endemic to Mount Cameroon, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, pale yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests on the mountain slopes.

children.name: Zosterops orbitalis
children.description: Found in the mountains of New Guinea, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, pale underparts, and an exceptionally broad white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests.

children.name: Zosterops caledonicus
children.description: Endemic to New Caledonia, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, pale grey underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits forests and scrub across the island.

children.name: Zosterops luketensis
children.description: Endemic to Socotra Island off the Horn of Africa, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, pale yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests and scrub.

children.name: Zosterops maculosus
children.description: Found in the mountains of Sulawesi, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, pale underparts with fine dark spotting, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests.

children.name: Zosterops griseipectus
children.description: Found on the island of Seram in the Moluccas, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, pale grey underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits lowland forests.

children.name: Zosterops coronatus
children.description: Found in the mountains of Sumatra, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, a distinctive yellow crown, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests.

children.name: Zosterops sulfureus
children.description: Found in the highlands of East Africa, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, bright sulphur-yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests and scrub.

children.name: Zosterops chalybeus
children.description: Found on islands in the Moluccas, this white-eye has grey upperparts, pale underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits lowland forests.

children.name: Zosterops flavus
children.description: Endemic to Java and Bali, this white-eye has bright yellow underparts, olive-green upperparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits lowland forests and gardens.

children.name: Zosterops chloronotus
children.description: Found in the mountains of Sumatra, this white-eye has bright olive-green upperparts, pale yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests.

children.name: Zosterops luteivirens
children.description: Endemic to the Philippines, this white-eye has yellowish-green upperparts, bright yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits lowland forests and scrub.

children.name: Zosterops aureiventris
children.description: Found in the mountains of New Guinea, this white-eye has golden-yellow underparts, olive-green upperparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests.

children.name: Zosterops brunneiventris
children.description: Found in the mountains of Seram in the Moluccas, this white-eye has brownish underparts, olive-green upperparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests.

children.name: Zosterops flavicollis
children.description: Found in the mountains of New Guinea, this white-eye has a bright yellow throat, olive-green upperparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests.

children.name: Zosterops griseiviridis
children.description: Found in the highlands of Tanzania, this white-eye has greyish-olive upperparts, pale underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests.

children.name: Zosterops olivaceiventris
children.description: Found in the highlands of eastern Africa, this white-eye has olive-tinged underparts, greyish-olive upperparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests.

children.name: Zosterops laticola
children.description: Found in the forests of Central Africa, this white-eye has a broad bill, olive-green upperparts, pale underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits lowland rainforests.

children.name: Zosterops monticolus
children.description: Found in the highlands of East Africa, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, pale grey underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests.

children.name: Zosterops albocingulatus
children.description: Found in the mountains of Sumatra, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, white underparts with a distinctive white band across the belly, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests.

children.name: Zosterops cinereiventris
children.description: Found on islands in the Moluccas, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, ash-grey underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits lowland forests.

children.name: Zosterops nigrosternus
children.description: Found in the mountains of Sulawesi, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, a distinctive black patch on the breast, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests.

children.name: Zosterops fusciventris
children.description: Found on islands in the Bismarck Archipelago, this white-eye has olive-green upperparts, dusky brown underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits lowland forests.

children.name: Zosterops albifrons
children.description: Found in the mountains of New Guinea, this white-eye has a white forehead, olive-green upperparts, pale underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests.

children.name: Zosterops rufiventris
children.description: Found in the mountains of Seram, this white-eye has rufous-tinged underparts, olive-green upperparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests.

children.name: Zosterops leucops
children.description: Found in the mountains of New Guinea, this white-eye has a white face, olive-green upperparts, pale underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests.

children.name: Zosterops chlorops
children.description: Found in the mountains of Borneo, this white-eye has a greenish face, bright olive upperparts, yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests.

children.name: Zosterops nigriceps
children.description: Found in the mountains of the Philippines, this white-eye has a black crown and nape, olive-green upperparts, white underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests.

children.name: Zosterops cinctiventris
children.description: Found in the mountains of New Guinea, this white-eye has a distinctive dark band across the belly, olive-green upperparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests.

children.name: Zosterops subcaeruleus
children.description: Found in the mountains of Sulawesi, this white-eye has bluish-tinged upperparts, pale underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests.

children.name: Zosterops chrysops
children.description: Found in the Solomon Islands, this white-eye has a golden-yellow face, olive-green upperparts, bright yellow underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits lowland forests.

children.name: Zosterops xanthogaster
children.description: Found in the mountains of New Guinea, this white-eye has bright yellow underparts, olive-green upperparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests.

children.name: Zosterops griseiceps
children.description: Found in the mountains of New Guinea, this white-eye has a grey crown, olive-green upperparts, pale underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests.

children.name: Zosterops flaviceps
children.description: Found in the mountains of Sulawesi, this white-eye has a yellow crown, olive-green upperparts, pale underparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests.

children.name: Zosterops rubriventris
children.description: Found in the mountains of New Guinea, this white-eye has a reddish-buff belly, olive-green upperparts, and a prominent white eye-ring. It inhabits montane forests.

children.name: Zosterops flavifrons alternus
children.description: A subspecies of the Yellow-fronted White-eye found on Tanna Island in Vanuatu, with more extensive yellow on the forehead and brighter underparts.

children.name: Zosterops minor lativirens
children.description: A subspecies of the Black-fronted White-eye found in the mountains of New Guinea, with greener upperparts and a broader black forehead patch.
